entities.name: PICK1
mentioned_activities: Modulates Arp2/3-mediated actin polymerization via Arf1

entities.name: Arp2/3
entities.context: Neuronal morphology and AMPA receptor endocytosis

interactions.quote: Inhibition of Arp2/3-mediated actin polymerization by PICK1 regulates neuronal morphology and AMPA receptor endocytosis.
interactions.confidence_note: Text describes direct inhibitory action on the activity of Arp2/3 complex.

interactions.quote: The small GTPase Arf1 modulates Arp2/3-mediated actin polymerization via PICK1 to regulate synaptic plasticity.
interactions.confidence_note: Arf1 acts via PICK1 to affect the target; the intermediate step is described.

interactions.quote: NSF ATPase and α-/β-SNAPs disassemble the AMPA receptor-PICK1 complex.
interactions.confidence_note: NSF is a known ATPase with direct chaperone/disassembly activity.

gaps: Only binding described for PICK1 and calcineurin B (Iida et al. 2008) — molecular function unknown.
Only binding described for PICK1 and GluR2/3 (Iwakura et al. 2001) — molecular function unknown.
Mechanism of how PICK1 'modulates' Arf1-Arp2/3 interaction is not detailed beyond the 'via' statement.
questions_for_expert: Does PICK1 act as a competitive inhibitor of Arp2/3 or does it sequester Arp2/3 subunits?
Is the PICK1-mediated modulation of Arp2/3 dependent on the calcium-binding state of PICK1 in the context of Arf1 signaling?
